mapping_note: Buildings 1-2 are allocated to G42/Core42 and Buildings 3-5 to Anthropic based on the center notes and timeline row descriptions.
timeline_status: Building 5 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB5 was expected to be energized by Q4 2026. However, based on the pace of Building 4, and comparisons to other projects like Stargate Abilene, we think this is optimistic. We expect a 3-month delay past the end of Q4 2026 to get to full operational capacity. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

mapping_note: Full center allocated to the single listed user.
timeline_status: Buildings 1-12 are operational. The outline of buildings 1-5 was evident on 2024-05-19 and estimated to be operational 1 year later. Assuming the same timeline, buildings 6-12 should be complete by 2026-09-18. This also roughly aligns with [expected completion by 2027](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-confirmed-as-company-behind-10bn-mississippi-data-center-development/)

timeline_status: Buildings 1-14 operational. Buildings 1-4 will likely take over 2 years on this site. By comparison, Buildings 1-7 at the New Carlisle site were completed in less than 1.5 years. This also roughly aligns with [expected completion by 2027](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-confirmed-as-company-behind-10bn-mississippi-data-center-development/)

mapping_note: Full center allocated to the single listed user.
timeline_status: Buildings 1-14 operational. Buildings 1-4 will likely take over 2 years on this site. By comparison, Buildings 1-7 at the New Carlisle site were completed in less than 1.5 years. Assuming a similar pace, we expect 10 additional buildings to take about 2 years after 2025-09-21. This aligns with [expected completion by 2027](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-confirmed-as-company-behind-10bn-mississippi-data-center-development/) and a [permit review document](https://opc.deq.state.ms.us/get_doc.aspx?dt=eparat&id=1747289) from 2025-06-25 for this site, stating the project will take “years” to build.

mapping_note: Full center allocated to the single listed user.
timeline_status: Buildings 17-18 are operational. This is an estimate based on a rule of thumb of "operational 5 months after roof is complete", assuming the roofs took one more month after 2025-12-23.

mapping_note: Buildings 1-2 are allocated to G42/Core42 and Buildings 3-5 to Anthropic based on the center notes and timeline row descriptions.
timeline_status: Building 5 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB5 was expected to be energized by Q4 2026. However, based on the pace of Building 4, and comparisons to other projects like Stargate Abilene, we think this is optimistic. We expect a 3-month delay past the end of Q4 2026 to get to full operational capacity. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

This aligns with [expected completion by 2027](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/aws-confirmed-as-company-behind-10bn-mississippi-data-center-development/) and a [permit review document](https://opc.deq.state.ms.us/get_doc.aspx?dt=eparat&id=1747289) from 2025-06-25 for this site, stating the project will take “years” to build.

mapping_note: Full center allocated to the single listed user.
timeline_status: Buildings 17-18 are operational. This is an estimate based on a rule of thumb of "operational 5 months after roof is complete", assuming the roofs took one more month after 2025-12-23.

mapping_note: Buildings 1-2 are allocated to G42/Core42 and Buildings 3-5 to Anthropic based on the center notes and timeline row descriptions.
timeline_status: Building 5 is operational. On February 26, Terawulf [stated](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/132/terawulf-reports-fourth-quarter-and-full-year-2025-results) that CB5 was expected to be energized by Q4 2026. However, based on the pace of Building 4, and comparisons to other projects like Stargate Abilene, we think this is optimistic. We expect a 3-month delay past the end of Q4 2026 to get to full operational capacity. This building is [leased](https://investors.terawulf.com/news-events/press-releases/detail/112/terawulf-signs-200-mw-10-year-ai-hosting-agreements-with) to Fluidstack, likely contains [Google TPUs](https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/google-offers-its-tpus-to-ai-cloud-providers-report/), and is likely used by [Anthropic](https://www.anthropic.com/news/anthropic-invests-50-billion-in-american-ai-infrastructure).

timeline_status: Building 1 and 2 fully operational, given the chillers look completely installed, the 200 MW substation built in 2022 is available, plus 5x 34-38 MW natural gas turbines looking complete. This is consistent with [Crusoe's impact report](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6855c1aa175582ee23e0aa19/68710273a71bb7c11049ba28_Crusoe_Impact_Report_2024.pdf#page=19) from June 2025 saying that 206 MW is expected to be energized in 2025 (unclear if IT power or total facility power, but this normally means IT power). 5 more turbines still under construction. Chillers partly installed for Building 3. Roof complete for Building 4. Larger 1 GW substation not complete yet.

timeline_status: Building 1 and 2 are [operational](https://news.microsoft.com/source/features/ai/from-wisconsin-to-atlanta-microsoft-connects-datacenters-to-build-its-first-ai-superfactory/). Building 1 looked operational sooner, but the buildings [are paired](https://blogs.microsoft.com/blog/2025/09/18/inside-the-worlds-most-powerful-ai-datacenter/) in the Fairwater design. The roof of Building 4 is almost complete, while mechanical equipment is still being installed.

The buildings here looked 3-6 months behind the Ajman building as of November 2025, but they were moving faster. So we think Building 1 and 2 are on track for 200 MW by December 2026, along with the Ajman building.

The similar data center in Ajman, UAE was delayed by over a year, now due in December 2026 (compare [1](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-expands-infrastructure-to-accelerate-uaes-leadership-in-artificial-intelligence/), [2](https://khaznadatacenters.com/press-release/khazna-announces-the-uaes-first-ai-optimized-data-center-and-the-largest-in-its-portfolio-powering-the-nations-drive-toward-ai-leadership/)). The buildings here looked 3-6 months behind the Ajman building as of November 2025, but they were moving faster. So we think Building 1 and 2 are on track for 200 MW by December 2026, along with the Ajman building.

timeline_status: 1 GW of IT power is operational. We haven’t seen expansion beyond 200 MW as of November 2025. Optimistically, 8 more 100 MW buildings start construction in December 2025, in parallel, and take 1.5 years like the first two buildings, hitting 1 GW by Q3 2027. More realistically, there's some staggering of construction over several months, like Stargate Abilene. Our median estimate is January 2028.

We assume it takes 3-6 months after that for all of the chips to be installed, commissioned, and served by Oracle to OpenAI.

mapping_note: Full center allocated to the single listed user.
timeline_status: 1 GW of IT power is operational. We haven’t seen expansion beyond 200 MW as of November 2025. Optimistically, 8 more 100 MW buildings start construction in December 2025, in parallel, and take 1.5 years like the first two buildings, hitting 1 GW by Q3 2027. More realistically, there's some staggering of construction over several months, like Stargate Abilene. Our median estimate is January 2028.
